content:
You are a programming expert. 
You will be provided with a patch used to fix a bug. 

Your task is summarize the modifications made in the patch.
Your summary should consist of two parts, modifications in each file and their relationships.

From 7f0221b904956359f2d739aa3a2b53f8c12ed8c7 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001 From: Mark Thomas Date: Wed, 3 Apr 2019 17:00:38 +0200 Subject: [PATCH] Limit CGI command line arguments Limit the decoded form of individual command line arguments. This is to work various issues passing command line arguments from Java to the OS on Windows. This restriction may be overridden by the new initialisation parameter cmdLineArgumentsDecoded. This is the fix for CVE-2019-0232. # Conflicts: # java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java diff --git a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties index 490763676e..35381f2afb 100644 --- a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties +++ b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties @@ -24,6 +24,7 @@ cgiServlet.expandOk=Expanded script at path [{0}] to [{1}] cgiServlet.find.found=Found CGI: name [{0}], path [{1}], script name [{2}] and CGI name [{3}] cgiServlet.find.location=Looking for a file at [{0}] cgiServlet.find.path=CGI script requested at path [{0}] relative to CGI location [{1}] +cgiServlet.invalidArgumentDecoded=The decoded command line argument [{0}] did not match the configured cmdLineArgumentsDecoded pattern [{1}] cgiServlet.invalidArgumentEncoded=The encoded command line argument [{0}] did not match the configured cmdLineArgumentsEncoded pattern [{1}] cgiServlet.runBadHeader=Bad header line [{0}] cgiServlet.runFail=I/O problems processing CGI diff --git a/conf/web.xml b/conf/web.xml index 10d4d27fc6..c2e44d953c 100644 --- a/conf/web.xml +++ b/conf/web.xml @@ -330,6 +330,20 @@ + + + + + + + + + + + + + + diff --git a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java index 470237f410..967a9d94c1 100644 --- a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java +++ b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java @@ -53,6 +53,7 @@ import javax.servlet.http.HttpSession; import org.apache.catalina.util.IOTools; import org.apache.juli.logging.Log; import org.apache.juli.logging.LogFactory; +import org.apache.naming.resources.JrePlatform; import org.apache.tomcat.util.res.StringManager; @@ -248,10 +249,21 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L; private static final Set DEFAULT_SUPER_METHODS = new HashSet(); + private static final Pattern DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN; + private static final String ALLOW_ANY_PATTERN = ".*"; + static { DEFAULT_SUPER_METHODS.add("HEAD"); DEFAULT_SUPER_METHODS.add("OPTIONS"); DEFAULT_SUPER_METHODS.add("TRACE"); + + if (JrePlatform.IS_WINDOWS) { + DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN = Pattern.compile("[a-zA-Z0-9\\Q-_.\\/:\\E]+"); + } else { + // No restrictions + DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN = null; + } + } @@ -317,6 +329,14 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { private Pattern cmdLineArgumentsEncodedPattern = Pattern.compile("[a-zA-Z0-9\\Q%;/?:@&,$-_.!~*'()\\E]+"); + /** + * Limits the decoded form of individual command line arguments. Default + * varies by platform. + */ + private Pattern cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern = DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN; + + + /** * Sets instance variables. *

@@ -414,6 +434,14 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { cmdLineArgumentsEncodedPattern = Pattern.compile(getServletConfig().getInitParameter("cmdLineArgumentsEncoded")); } + + String value = getServletConfig().getInitParameter("cmdLineArgumentsDecoded"); + if (ALLOW_ANY_PATTERN.equals(value)) { + // Optimisation for case where anything is allowed + cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern = null; + } else if (value != null) { + cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern = Pattern.compile(value); + } } @@ -817,7 +845,17 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { } return false; } + String decodedArgument = URLDecoder.decode(encodedArgument, parameterEncoding); + if (cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern != null && + !cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern.matcher(decodedArgument).matches()) { + if (log.isDebugEnabled()) { + log.debug(sm.getString("cgiServlet.invalidArgumentDecoded", + decodedArgument, cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern.toString())); + } + return false; + } + cmdLineParameters.add(decodedArgument); } } @@ -1126,7 +1164,6 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { this.env = envp; return true; - } /** diff --git a/webapps/docs/changelog.xml b/webapps/docs/changelog.xml index 4d9acd564c..6b3a032273 100644 --- a/webapps/docs/changelog.xml +++ b/webapps/docs/changelog.xml @@ -144,6 +144,14 @@ 3875. This restriction may be relaxed by the use of the new initialisation parameter cmdLineArgumentsEncoded. (markt) + + When the CGI Servlet is configured with + enableCmdLineArguments set to true, limit the decoded form + of the individual command line arguments to known safe values when + running on Windows. This restriction may be relaxed by the use of the + new initialisation parameter cmdLineArgumentsDecoded. This + is the fix for CVE-2019-0232. (markt) + diff --git a/webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml b/webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml index 4661f9349d..0fb86bead2 100644 --- a/webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml +++ b/webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml @@ -102,12 +102,22 @@ the web application root directory + File.separator + this prefix. By default there is no value, which results in the web application root directory being used as the search path. The recommended value is WEB-INF/cgi +

  • cmdLineArgumentsDecoded - If command line argumemnts +are enabled (via enableCmdLineArguments) and Tomcat is running +on Windows then each individual decoded command line argument must match this +pattern else the request will be rejected. This is to protect against known +issues passing command line arguments from Java to Windows. These issues can +lead to remote code execution. For more information on these issues see +Markus +Wulftange's blog and this archived +blog +by Daniel Colascione.
  • cmdLineArgumentsEncoded - If command line argumemnts are enabled (via enableCmdLineArguments) individual encoded command line argument must match this pattern else the request will be rejected. The default matches the allowed values defined by RFC3875 and is [a-zA-Z0-9\Q%;/?:@&,$-_.!~*'()\E]+
    • -
  • enableCmdLineArguments - Are command line parameters +
  • enableCmdLineArguments - Are command line arguments generated from the query string as per section 4.4 of 3875 RFC? The default is true.
  • environment-variable- - An environment to be set for the diff --git a/webapps/docs/security-howto.xml b/webapps/docs/security-howto.xml index 31934b2f2a..484272fe9d 100644 --- a/webapps/docs/security-howto.xml +++ b/webapps/docs/security-howto.xml @@ -503,6 +503,14 @@ server.info=Apache Tomcat/7.0.x initialisation parameter should not be set to 10 or higher on a production system because the debug page is not secure.

    +

    When using the CGI Servlet on Windows with + enableCmdLineArguments enabled, review the setting of + cmdLineArgumentsDecoded carefully and ensure that it is + appropriate for your environment. The default value is secure. Insecure + configurations may expose the server to remote code execution. Further + information on the potential risks and mitigations may be found by + following the links in the CGI How To.

    +

    FailedRequestFilter can be configured and used to reject requests that had errors during request parameter parsing. Without the filter the default behaviour is -- 2.34.1

### Modifications in Each File

1. **LocalStrings.properties**
   - Added a new string entry: `cgiServlet.invalidArgumentDecoded`, which provides a message for when a decoded command line argument does not match the configured pattern.

2. **web.xml**
   - Added documentation comments for the new initialization parameter `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded`, explaining its purpose and default values based on the platform.

3. **CGIServlet.java**
   - Introduced a new pattern `cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern` to limit the decoded form of individual command line arguments, with a default pattern based on the operating system (Windows vs. others).
   - Updated the initialization method to read the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter and compile it into a pattern if provided.
   - Added validation logic to check if decoded command line arguments match the specified pattern, logging a debug message if they do not.

4. **changelog.xml**
   - Added an entry to the changelog detailing the new restriction on decoded command line arguments when using the CGI Servlet on Windows and the introduction of the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter.

5. **cgi-howto.xml**
   - Updated documentation to explain the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter, its purpose, and the security implications of using it on Windows.

6. **security-howto.xml**
   - Added a note regarding the use of the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter when using the CGI Servlet on Windows, emphasizing the need for careful configuration to avoid security risks.

### Relationships Between Modifications

The modifications across the files are interconnected as follows:

- The **CGIServlet.java** file implements the core functionality of limiting command line arguments based on the new `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter, which is introduced in the **web.xml** configuration file. This parameter allows users to specify a regex pattern for validating decoded command line arguments, enhancing security against potential command injection vulnerabilities on Windows systems.

- The **LocalStrings.properties** file provides user-friendly messages that are logged when the validation fails, improving the debugging experience for developers.

- The **changelog.xml**, **cgi-howto.xml**, and **security-howto.xml** files document the changes and provide guidance on the new parameter, ensuring that users are aware of the security implications and how to configure the servlet correctly.

Overall, the modifications collectively enhance the security of the CGI Servlet by introducing a mechanism to validate command line arguments, particularly addressing vulnerabilities specific to Windows environments.

Based on your summary and the commit message in the patch, you need to:

1. analyze the root cause of the bug in detail. 
2. output the file name that is most likely to cause the bug. 



Provide a response strictly in the following format:
Root cause:<Root cause>
file:<file name>

1. analyze the root cause of the bug in detail. 
2. output the file name that is most likely to cause the bug. 



Provide a response strictly in the following format:
Root cause:<Root cause>
file:<file name>

You will be provided with a patch used to fix a bug. 

Your task is summarize the modifications made in the patch.
Your summary should consist of two parts, modifications in each file and their relationships.

From 7f0221b904956359f2d739aa3a2b53f8c12ed8c7 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001 From: Mark Thomas Date: Wed, 3 Apr 2019 17:00:38 +0200 Subject: [PATCH] Limit CGI command line arguments Limit the decoded form of individual command line arguments. This is to work various issues passing command line arguments from Java to the OS on Windows. This restriction may be overridden by the new initialisation parameter cmdLineArgumentsDecoded. This is the fix for CVE-2019-0232. # Conflicts: # java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java diff --git a/webapps/docs/changelog.xml b/webapps/docs/changelog.xml index 4d9acd564c..6b3a032273 100644 --- a/webapps/docs/changelog.xml +++ b/webapps/docs/changelog.xml @@ -144,6 +144,14 @@ 3875. This restriction may be relaxed by the use of the new initialisation parameter cmdLineArgumentsEncoded. (markt) + + When the CGI Servlet is configured with + enableCmdLineArguments set to true, limit the decoded form + of the individual command line arguments to known safe values when + running on Windows. This restriction may be relaxed by the use of the + new initialisation parameter cmdLineArgumentsDecoded. This + is the fix for CVE-2019-0232. (markt) + diff --git a/webapps/docs/security-howto.xml b/webapps/docs/security-howto.xml index 31934b2f2a..484272fe9d 100644 --- a/webapps/docs/security-howto.xml +++ b/webapps/docs/security-howto.xml @@ -503,6 +503,14 @@ server.info=Apache Tomcat/7.0.x initialisation parameter should not be set to 10 or higher on a production system because the debug page is not secure.

+

When using the CGI Servlet on Windows with + enableCmdLineArguments enabled, review the setting of + cmdLineArgumentsDecoded carefully and ensure that it is + appropriate for your environment. The default value is secure. Insecure + configurations may expose the server to remote code execution. Further + information on the potential risks and mitigations may be found by + following the links in the CGI How To.

+

FailedRequestFilter can be configured and used to reject requests that had errors during request parameter parsing. Without the filter the default behaviour is -- 2.34.1 diff --git a/webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml b/webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml index 4661f9349d..0fb86bead2 100644 --- a/webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml +++ b/webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml @@ -102,12 +102,22 @@ the web application root directory + File.separator + this prefix. By default there is no value, which results in the web application root directory being used as the search path. The recommended value is WEB-INF/cgi +

  • cmdLineArgumentsDecoded - If command line argumemnts +are enabled (via enableCmdLineArguments) and Tomcat is running +on Windows then each individual decoded command line argument must match this +pattern else the request will be rejected. This is to protect against known +issues passing command line arguments from Java to Windows. These issues can +lead to remote code execution. For more information on these issues see +Markus +Wulftange's blog and this archived +blog +by Daniel Colascione.
  • cmdLineArgumentsEncoded - If command line argumemnts are enabled (via enableCmdLineArguments) individual encoded command line argument must match this pattern else the request will be rejected. The default matches the allowed values defined by RFC3875 and is [a-zA-Z0-9\Q%;/?:@&,$-_.!~*'()\E]+
    • -
  • enableCmdLineArguments - Are command line parameters +
  • enableCmdLineArguments - Are command line arguments generated from the query string as per section 4.4 of 3875 RFC? The default is true.
  • environment-variable- - An environment to be set for the diff --git a/conf/web.xml b/conf/web.xml index 10d4d27fc6..c2e44d953c 100644 --- a/conf/web.xml +++ b/conf/web.xml @@ -330,6 +330,20 @@ + + + + + + + + + + + + + + diff --git a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties index 490763676e..35381f2afb 100644 --- a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties +++ b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties @@ -24,6 +24,7 @@ cgiServlet.expandOk=Expanded script at path [{0}] to [{1}] cgiServlet.find.found=Found CGI: name [{0}], path [{1}], script name [{2}] and CGI name [{3}] cgiServlet.find.location=Looking for a file at [{0}] cgiServlet.find.path=CGI script requested at path [{0}] relative to CGI location [{1}] +cgiServlet.invalidArgumentDecoded=The decoded command line argument [{0}] did not match the configured cmdLineArgumentsDecoded pattern [{1}] cgiServlet.invalidArgumentEncoded=The encoded command line argument [{0}] did not match the configured cmdLineArgumentsEncoded pattern [{1}] cgiServlet.runBadHeader=Bad header line [{0}] cgiServlet.runFail=I/O problems processing CGI diff --git a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java index 470237f410..967a9d94c1 100644 --- a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java +++ b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java @@ -53,6 +53,7 @@ import javax.servlet.http.HttpSession; import org.apache.catalina.util.IOTools; import org.apache.juli.logging.Log; import org.apache.juli.logging.LogFactory; +import org.apache.naming.resources.JrePlatform; import org.apache.tomcat.util.res.StringManager; @@ -248,10 +249,21 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L; private static final Set DEFAULT_SUPER_METHODS = new HashSet(); + private static final Pattern DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN; + private static final String ALLOW_ANY_PATTERN = ".*"; + static { DEFAULT_SUPER_METHODS.add("HEAD"); DEFAULT_SUPER_METHODS.add("OPTIONS"); DEFAULT_SUPER_METHODS.add("TRACE"); + + if (JrePlatform.IS_WINDOWS) { + DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN = Pattern.compile("[a-zA-Z0-9\\Q-_.\\/:\\E]+"); + } else { + // No restrictions + DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN = null; + } + } @@ -317,6 +329,14 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { private Pattern cmdLineArgumentsEncodedPattern = Pattern.compile("[a-zA-Z0-9\\Q%;/?:@&,$-_.!~*'()\\E]+"); + /** + * Limits the decoded form of individual command line arguments. Default + * varies by platform. + */ + private Pattern cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern = DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN; + + + /** * Sets instance variables. *

    @@ -414,6 +434,14 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { cmdLineArgumentsEncodedPattern = Pattern.compile(getServletConfig().getInitParameter("cmdLineArgumentsEncoded")); } + + String value = getServletConfig().getInitParameter("cmdLineArgumentsDecoded"); + if (ALLOW_ANY_PATTERN.equals(value)) { + // Optimisation for case where anything is allowed + cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern = null; + } else if (value != null) { + cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern = Pattern.compile(value); + } } @@ -817,7 +845,17 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { } return false; } + String decodedArgument = URLDecoder.decode(encodedArgument, parameterEncoding); + if (cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern != null && + !cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern.matcher(decodedArgument).matches()) { + if (log.isDebugEnabled()) { + log.debug(sm.getString("cgiServlet.invalidArgumentDecoded", + decodedArgument, cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern.toString())); + } + return false; + } + cmdLineParameters.add(decodedArgument); } } @@ -1126,7 +1164,6 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { this.env = envp; return true; - } /**

### Modifications in Each File

1. **`webapps/docs/changelog.xml`**:
   - Added a new entry to the changelog detailing the limitation on decoded command line arguments for the CGI Servlet when running on Windows. It mentions the new initialization parameter `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` and references the fix for CVE-2019-0232.

2. **`webapps/docs/security-howto.xml`**:
   - Included a paragraph advising users to review the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` setting when using the CGI Servlet on Windows with `enableCmdLineArguments` enabled. It warns about potential security risks, including remote code execution.

3. **`webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml`**:
   - Added a description for the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter, explaining its purpose and the security implications of command line arguments on Windows.

4. **`conf/web.xml`**:
   - Added comments regarding the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter, explaining its usage and the default patterns for Windows and other platforms.

5. **`java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties`**:
   - Introduced a new string for logging invalid decoded command line arguments that do not match the configured pattern.

6. **`java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java`**:
   - Added logic to define a default pattern for decoded command line arguments based on the operating system (Windows vs. others).
   - Introduced a new instance variable `cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern` to hold the pattern for decoded command line arguments.
   - Updated the initialization method to read the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter and compile it into a pattern.
   - Implemented validation logic to check if decoded command line arguments match the specified pattern, logging a debug message if they do not.

### Relationships Between Modifications

The modifications across the files are interconnected, primarily focusing on enhancing the security of command line arguments in the CGI Servlet on Windows. The documentation updates in `changelog.xml`, `security-howto.xml`, and `cgi-howto.xml` provide context and guidance for users regarding the new `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter. 

The changes in `web.xml` offer a configuration reference for this parameter, while the updates in `LocalStrings.properties` ensure that any invalid arguments are logged appropriately. The core logic in `CGIServlet.java` implements the actual functionality of limiting decoded command line arguments based on the specified patterns, thereby addressing the security vulnerability (CVE-2019-0232) and ensuring that the application behaves securely when handling command line arguments. 

Overall, the patch establishes a cohesive framework for managing command line arguments securely, with clear documentation and implementation details that guide users in configuring their systems appropriately.

Default + * varies by platform. + */ + private Pattern cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern = DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN; + + + /** * Sets instance variables. *

### Modifications in Each File

1. **`webapps/docs/security-howto.xml`**:
   - Added a paragraph explaining the implications of using the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter when the CGI Servlet is enabled on Windows. It emphasizes the need for careful review of this setting to avoid potential security risks, including remote code execution.

2. **`java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java`**:
   - Introduced a new pattern `DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN` to limit the decoded form of command line arguments on Windows.
   - Added logic to set `cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern` based on the platform (restrictive for Windows, no restrictions for others).
   - Implemented validation to check if decoded command line arguments match the specified pattern, logging a debug message if they do not.

3. **`webapps/docs/changelog.xml`**:
   - Documented the new feature that limits the decoded form of command line arguments when the CGI Servlet is enabled on Windows, mentioning the new initialization parameter `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` and its purpose as a fix for CVE-2019-0232.

4. **`java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties`**:
   - Added a new string for logging invalid decoded command line arguments, providing a message format for debugging.

5. **`webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml`**:
   - Updated the documentation to include details about the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter, explaining its role in limiting decoded command line arguments on Windows and linking to external resources for further information on the associated risks.

6. **`conf/web.xml`**:
   - Added comments regarding the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter, explaining its usage, default values, and the rationale behind its implementation.

### Relationships Between Modifications

The modifications across the files are interconnected, primarily focusing on enhancing the security of the CGI Servlet when handling command line arguments on Windows. The changes in `CGIServlet.java` introduce the core functionality to limit and validate command line arguments, while the updates in the documentation files (`security-howto.xml`, `changelog.xml`, `cgi-howto.xml`, and `web.xml`) provide necessary context, usage instructions, and warnings about potential security risks. This cohesive approach ensures that developers are informed about the new parameter and its implications, thereby promoting secure configurations in their applications.
